# OpenAPI Schema Enhancement Guide

**Purpose**: When autogenerated OpenAPI spec lacks sufficient detail, use this guide to manually enhance schemas.

**Effective Date**: 2026-04-06  
**Related**: SWAGGER_CONVENTION.md, See SPEC_CANONICAL.md for data contract definitions

---

## When Cannot Use JSDoc Alone

JSDoc/swagger-autogen works well for:
- Simple parameter types
- Basic request/response shapes
- Standard HTTP status codes

**But fails for**:
- Complex nested objects
- Multi-language JSONB fields
- Conditional fields based on user role
- Discriminated unions (polymorphic types)
- Custom validation rules

---

## Solution: Post-Processing Enhancement

### Option 1: Extend `openapi-postprocess.cjs`

Add schema enhancement section to `scripts/openapi-postprocess.cjs`:

```javascript
// After basic processing, add detailed schemas:
doc.components.schemas.PointOfInterest = {
  type: 'object',
  required: ['id', 'name', 'location'],
  properties: {
    id: {
      type: 'string',
      format: 'uuid',
      description: 'Unique POI identifier'
    },
    name: {
      type: 'object',
      description: 'Multi-language names (JSONB)',
      properties: {
        vi: { type: 'string', example: 'Phở Thìn' },
        en: { type: 'string', example: 'Pho Thin' },
        ko: { type: 'string', example: '포 띠인' }
        // ... 12 more languages
      }
    },
    audioUrls: {
      type: 'object',
      description: 'Pre-generated MP3 URLs (JSONB)',
      properties: {
        vi: { type: 'string', format: 'uri' },
        en: { type: 'string', format: 'uri' }
        // ... per language
      }
    },
    location: {
      type: 'object',
      required: ['latitude', 'longitude'],
      properties: {
        latitude: { type: 'number', format: 'double', minimum: -90, maximum: 90 },
        longitude: { type: 'number', format: 'double', minimum: -180, maximum: 180 }
      }
    },
    premium: {
      type: 'boolean',
      description: 'Whether content is restricted to premium users'
    }
  }
};

// Reference it in response schemas:
doc.paths['/api/v1/pois/{id}'].get.responses['200'].content['application/json'].schema = {
  type: 'object',
  properties: {
    status: { type: 'string', enum: ['success'] },
    data: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/PointOfInterest' }
  }
};
```

**Advantages**:
- Stays in single generation pipeline
- No manual editing of `openapi.json`
- Changes tracked in version control
- Easy to refactor across endpoints

**Disadvantages**:
- Postprocess file gets large
- Hard to maintain with many schemas

---

### Option 2: Separate Schema Definition File

Create `apps/backend/schemas/openapi-schemas.js`:

```javascript
module.exports = {
  PointOfInterest: {
    type: 'object',
    // ... detailed definition
  },
  Tour: {
    type: 'object',
    // ... detailed definition
  },
  // ... more schemas
};
```

Extend postprocess to load and merge:

```javascript
// In openapi-postprocess.cjs
const customSchemas = require('./schemas/openapi-schemas.js');
doc.components.schemas = { ...doc.components.schemas, ...customSchemas };
```

**Advantages**:
- Schemas in separate file, easier to navigate
- Reusable across multiple specs
- Easy to sync with backend TypeScript types

**Disadvantages**:
- Extra file to maintain
- Risk of drift with TypeScript types

---

## Key Data Models to Define

Based on SPEC_CANONICAL.md and database_design.md:

### 1. PointOfInterest

```typescript
{
  id: UUID
  name: JSONB ({ vi, en, ko, ... })
  description: JSONB
  audioUrls: JSONB ({ vi: "/audio/poi_001_vi.mp3", ... })
  latitude: Decimal
  longitude: Decimal
  imageUrl?: string (Cloudinary)
  premium: boolean
  tourIds: string[]
  createdAt: ISO8601
  updatedAt: ISO8601
}
```

### 2. Tour

```typescript
{
  id: UUID
  name: JSONB ({ vi, en, ... })
  description: JSONB
  imageUrl?: string
  poiSequence: UUID[]  // ordered list of POI IDs
  premium: boolean
  createdAt: ISO8601
  updatedAt: ISO8601
}
```

### 3. SyncResponse

```typescript
{
  status: 'success' | 'error'
  data: {
    serverVersion: integer
    dataChecksum: string (sha256)
    lastUpdated: ISO8601
    requiresFullSync: boolean
    pois?: PointOfInterest[]     // only in full sync
    tours?: Tour[]               // only in full sync
  }
}
```

### 4. UserProfile

```typescript
{
  id: UUID
  email: string
  isPremium: boolean
  preferences: {
    language: string ('vi' | 'en' | ...)
    newsletter: boolean
  }
  createdAt: ISO8601
}
```

---

## When to Apply Enhancement

### Trigger 1: PR Review Finds Schema Mismatch

Example: Spec says `{ email: string }` but code returns `{ email: string, createdAt: ISO8601 }`

**Fix**:
```javascript
// Add to postprocess or schemas file
doc.paths['/auth/register'].post.responses['201'].content['application/json'].schema = {
  type: 'object',
  required: ['status', 'data'],
  properties: {
    status: { type: 'string', enum: ['success'] },
    data: {
      type: 'object',
      properties: {
        userId: { type: 'string', format: 'uuid' },
        email: { type: 'string', format: 'email' },
        createdAt: { type: 'string', format: 'date-time' }
      }
    }
  }
};
```

### Trigger 2: Mobile Client Can't Parse Response

Example: Mobile developer says "I don't know how to handle audioUrls because spec doesn't show structure"

**Fix**: Add JSONB schema definition (see PointOfInterest above)

### Trigger 3: API Behavior Changed

Example: New field `premium` added to `PointOfInterest`

**Flow**:
1. Backend code changes route/service
2. Engineer updates JSDoc: `@return {object} - Added 'premium' field`
3. Run `npm run openapi:generate`
4. If postprocess has custom schema, manually enhance
5. Commit both code + spec changes

---

## Testing & Validation

After schema enhancement:

```bash
# 1. Validate JSON syntax
npm run openapi:check

# 2. Lint against rules
npm run openapi:lint

# 3. Manually verify schema
# Open http://localhost:3000/api-docs and check:
# - Response examples look correct
# - Required fields marked properly
# - Field descriptions clear

# 4. Test with real requests
npm test
npm run test:integration  # if available
```

---

## PR Review Checklist for Schema Enhancement

Reviewer must verify:
- [ ] Schema matches actual response (test with real request)
- [ ] All required fields marked with `required: [...]`
- [ ] Field types correct (`string`, `number`, `boolean`, `array`, `object`)
- [ ] Format specified where applicable (`email`, `uuid`, `date-time`, `uri`)
- [ ] Descriptions are clear and non-empty
- [ ] Examples are realistic
- [ ] JSONB fields show nested structure (not just `type: 'object'`)
- [ ] Optional fields documented as such
- [ ] `npm run openapi:lint` passes

---

## Common Schema Patterns

### Multi-Language Field

```javascript
{
  type: 'object',
  description: '15-language content (JSONB)',
  additionalProperties: true, // allows any language code
  properties: {
    vi: { type: 'string', example: 'Phở Thìn' },
    en: { type: 'string', example: 'Pho Thin' },
    ko: { type: 'string', example: '포 띠인' }
    // Include 3-4 common ones as examples, rest implicit
  }
}
```

### Pagination Response

```javascript
{
  type: 'object',
  properties: {
    status: { type: 'string', enum: ['success'] },
    data: {
      type: 'array',
      items: { /* ItemSchema */ }
    },
    meta: {
      type: 'object',
      properties: {
        total: { type: 'integer' },
        page: { type: 'integer' },
        limit: { type: 'integer' },
        hasMore: { type: 'boolean' }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Geospatial Field (PostGIS)

```javascript
{
  type: 'object',
  description: 'Geographic location',
  required: ['latitude', 'longitude'],
  properties: {
    latitude: {
      type: 'number',
      format: 'double',
      minimum: -90,
      maximum: 90,
      description: 'WGS84 latitude'
    },
    longitude: {
      type: 'number',
      format: 'double',
      minimum: -180,
      maximum: 180,
      description: 'WGS84 longitude'
    }
  }
}
```

### Error Response

```javascript
{
  type: 'object',
  properties: {
    status: { type: 'string', enum: ['error'] },
    error: {
      type: 'object',
      properties: {
        code: {
          type: 'string',
          enum: ['VALIDATION_ERROR', 'UNAUTHORIZED', 'NOT_FOUND', 'INTERNAL_ERROR']
        },
        message: { type: 'string' },
        timestamp: { type: 'string', format: 'date-time' }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

---
